front: Poem: 'An ecstasy of fumbling' follows 'Gas! Gas! Quick, boys!' — soldiers scrambling for gas helmets
Poem: dying soldier 'plunges at me, guttering, choking, drowning' in speaker's dreams
Poem: blood from 'froth-corrupted lungs' — 'obscene as cancer / bitter as the cud'
Poem: 'each slow dusk a drawing-down of blinds' — closing image
Poem: 'the monstrous anger of the guns' and 'the stuttering rifles' rapid rattle'
Poem: 'shrill, demented choirs' mourn men
Poem: eyes 'shine the holy glimmers of good-byes'
Poem: Sassoon suggested the title and phrase 'tenderness of patient minds'
Poem: 'the pity of war, the pity war distilled' / 'the truth untold'
Poem: preface: 'the Poetry is in the pity'
Poem: 'I am the enemy you killed, my friend' / 'Let us sleep now' — dead enemy in tunnel
Poem: tunnel 'scooped through granites which titanic wars had groined'
Poem: 'courage was mine, and I had mystery'
Poem: Abraham ignores angel's command to 'Offer the Ram of Pride instead of him' — goes through with sacrifice of Isaac
back: The Parable of the Young Man and the Old (Wilfred Owen)
work: The Parable of the Young Man and the Old

front: Author: described Sassoon as 'Keats + Christ + Elijah + my Colonel + my father-confessor + Amenophis IV'
Author: met Sassoon at Craiglockhart Hospital
Author: fictionalized in Pat Barker's Regeneration
Author: 'Insensibility' — 'Happy are men who yet before they are killed can let their veins run cold'
Author: curses those immune to 'the eternal reciprocity of tears'
Author: 'Mental Cases' — 'men whose minds the Dead have ravished'
Author: 'Arms and the Boy' — teeth 'seem for laughing round an apple'; 'God will grow no talons'
Poem: originally dedicated to Jessie Pope (pro-war poet of "Who's for the Game?")
Poem: "watch the white eyes writhing in his face / His hanging face, like a devil's sick of sin"
Poem: "All went lame; all blind; drunk with fatigue"
